content.problemStatement:
Chef built a binary string SSS that has exactly NNN hills and MMM valleys.



A **hill** is any index 1<i<∣S∣1 \lt i \lt |S|1<i<∣S∣ such that both its neighbors are strictly smaller than it, i.e, Si−1<SiS_{i-1} \lt S_iSi−1​<Si​ and Si+1<SiS_{i+1} \lt S_iSi+1​<Si​.




A **valley** is any index 1<i<∣S∣1 \lt i \lt |S|1<i<∣S∣ such that both its neighbors are strictly larger than it, i.e, Si−1>SiS_{i-1} \gt S_iSi−1​>Si​ and Si+1>SiS_{i+1} \gt S_iSi+1​>Si​.



Chef thinks that his string SSS is the shortest among all binary strings with NNN hills and MMM valleys.  You don't quite trust his words, so to verify his claim you would like to find the shortest binary string with exactly NNN hills and MMM valleys.

If there are multiple possible binary strings of the least length satisfying the given condition, you may print any of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM, the required number of hills and valleys respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output two lines.



The first line contains the length of the binary string you constructed to satisfy the given conditions.
The second line contains the string itself.
constraints: The sum of lengths of the answer strings across all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105

You may output any string of length not exceeding 320320320 containing exactly NNN hills and MMM valleys:  it **need not be shortest by length**. It is guaranteed that at least one such string with length ≤320\leq 320≤320 exists.
The binary string you construct **must be shortest by length**.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The given string has hills at indices 2,4,62, 4, 62,4,6 and valleys at indices 3,53, 53,5. It can be verified that there is no string of length 666 or less with 333 hills and 222 valleys.  Note that for subtask 111, a binary string like 001010100001010100001010100 will also be accepted as a valid output, even though it is not shortest by length.
Test case 333: Apart from the given string, another possible string of length 888 that has 333 hills and 333 valleys is 101010101010101010101010. You may print any of them.
